title: British Turkey Meatball Bowl
description: A classic British-inspired dish featuring turkey meatballs served in a bowl with a tangy sauce.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine the turkey mince, crumbly bread, egg, and a pinch of salt and pepper. Mix well with your hands until just combined.
Use your hands to shape the mixture into small meatballs, about 1 1/2 inches (3.8 cm) in diameter.
Place the meatballs on a baking tray lined with parchment paper, leaving a little space between each meatball.
Drizzle the meatballs with a little milk and gently roll them to coat evenly.
Bake the meatballs in the preheated oven for 18-20 minutes, or until cooked through.
While the meatballs are baking, heat a little oil in a pan over medium heat.
Add the diced bitter onion to the pan and cook for 5-7 minutes, or until softened and lightly browned.
Once the meatballs are done, carefully place them in the bowl with the onion and sauce.
Serve hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
